findings.implication: Applicant CV: Lists formal qualifications (MRCP, PGDip) and a research grant as Lead Applicant, demonstrating relevant training and experience.
Applicant CV: Extensive publication record in high-impact journals and multiple awards (e.g., UKDCTN Fellowship, Michael Hornstein Memorial Scholarship) confirm impactful outputs.
Applicant CV: Progression from Foundation Doctor to Specialty Registrar and Lead Applicant on grants indicates increasing research responsibility.
Applicant CV: Roles as Academic Clinical Fellow and Specialty Registrar in Dermatology alongside research grants demonstrate a clear practitioner-academic trajectory.
Applicant Research Background: Clearly articulates a research vision to become an independent Clinician Scientist and outlines a trajectory from DRF to Clinical Lectureship.
Applicant Research Background: Explains the DRF as a natural next step to hone specific interests and position for a Clinical Lectureship, highlighting the need for dedicated research time.
Applicant Research Background: Details extensive experience in systematic reviews, multicentre studies, single-cell RNA sequencing, and formal training in study design and statistics (R, Stata).
Applicant Research Background: Lists 14 peer-reviewed publications (8 first author), an h-index of 9, and multiple awards including a UKDCTN fellowship and travel scholarships.
Applicant Research Background: Demonstrates increasing responsibility by leading projects from conception, chairing meetings, serving on guideline groups, and acting as a peer reviewer.
Applicant Research Background: Explicitly states aspiration to be an academic dermatologist and provides evidence of balancing clinical registrar duties with significant research outputs.
Applicant Research Background: Mentions organizing a PPI advisory event and refining the proposal based on feedback from PPI collaborators, indicating integration of lived experience.
Plain English Summary of Research: The section clearly outlines the problem, aim, methods, and impact in accessible language, demonstrating high quality and readability for a non-specialist audience.
Plain English Summary of Research: The background identifies a specific knowledge gap: the inability to predict long-term treatment response in psoriasis, justifying the proposed research.

Plain English Summary of Research: The methods describe a feasible sequence of tasks (literature review, model building using existing datasets, stakeholder consultation) appropriate for a research award.

Plain English Summary of Research: The section details specific methodological steps including data sources (UK/Europe datasets), variables (age, ethnicity, genetics), and iterative tool development with feedback.
Plain English Summary of Research: The text explicitly states benefits for patients (reduced worry, personalized care) and the NHS (efficient resource use), aligning with health priorities.

Plain English Summary of Research: Strong evidence of engagement is provided, including initial meetings, 6-monthly involvement, and specific roles for patients in planning and analyzing group discussions.
Plain English Summary of Research: The applicant explicitly commits to ensuring the research is inclusive and relevant to diverse communities through patient involvement.
Plain English Summary of Research: The design incorporates feedback from a meeting with people with psoriasis, confirming the integration of lived experience into the research design.
Plain English Summary of Research: A clear dissemination plan is outlined, including conferences, journals, and patient-friendly summaries circulated via charities and social media.

Plain English Summary of Research: Patient involvement is planned across multiple stages, from initial design feedback to planning discussions and analyzing conversations, ensuring inclusivity across the lifecycle.
Scientific Abstract: Identifies a clear knowledge gap regarding the inability to predict long-term disease control in psoriasis, justifying the proposed systematic review and modeling.
Scientific Abstract: Outlines a feasible 36-month timeline with distinct phases (review, modeling, refinement) and specific tasks, indicating appropriate scope for the award.
Scientific Abstract: Describes rigorous methods including systematic review, validation using large-scale real-world datasets from UK/Europe, and exploration of genetic data.
Scientific Abstract: Details specific patient benefits (reduced anxiety, autonomy) and health service impacts (efficient resource allocation, remote monitoring pathways).
Scientific Abstract: Describes integral PPI involvement throughout the project, including recruitment strategies, co-facilitating focus groups, and qualitative analysis.
Scientific Abstract: Explicitly states that PPI collaborators will ensure the research is inclusive and relevant to diverse communities.
Scientific Abstract: Demonstrates integration of lived experience through PPI input on risk categories, user interface design, and co-development of dissemination materials.
Scientific Abstract: Outlines a robust dissemination plan including peer-reviewed publications, conferences, and patient-friendly summaries shared via associations and social media.
Scientific Abstract: Shows inclusivity across the lifecycle with PPI involvement in design, recruitment, analysis, and dissemination planning.

Patient & Public Involvement: Proposal refined based on PPI advisory group feedback; specific design changes include outcome definition (≥6 months), genetic data inclusion, and Phase 3 co-design.
Patient & Public Involvement: PPI group recruited for diversity in age, ethnicity, location, and disease duration; future group includes patients and carers to ensure diverse views.
Patient & Public Involvement: Dissemination plan includes patient-friendly summaries, social media, and PPI webinars; PPI collaborators invited to co-author and co-present.
Patient & Public Involvement: Strategies to recruit underserved groups in Phase 3; measures to reduce digital exclusion via phone-in options and practice calls.
Patient & Public Involvement: PPI involvement spans proposal refinement, variable selection, recruitment strategy, focus group co-facilitation, and interpretation of findings.
Patient & Public Involvement: Budget explicitly includes PPI remuneration and expenses per NIHR guidance; GRIPP2 guidelines will be used for reporting.
Patient & Public Involvement: PPI collaborators will attend training sessions on PPI and focus group facilitation; additional training provided for qualitative data interpretation.
